# Car Analysis Instructions

When analyzing a car model, treat each trim or variant as a separate data point whenever the market actually differentiates it.

Use English field labels in guidance and structured matrices for consistency, but keep the final repository content aligned with the repo's Indonesian writing style.

If a detail is only available at model level, say that clearly instead of guessing. If a feature name differs by brand, map it to a common category and also mention the original branded term.

Prioritize Indonesian sources and Indonesian owner feedback. Reviews, quotes, and community evidence should come from Indonesian sources whenever available.

## Core Output Structure

Include the following sections.

## Overview

Give a short explanation of the vehicle, its target buyer, and its market positioning in Indonesia.

## Variant-by-Variant Data Matrix

For each variant, collect and present as much of the following as possible:

- price
- release date
- dimensions
- ground clearance
- luggage capacity
- fuel tank capacity
- engine specifications
- horsepower / PS / power if available
- torque if available
- transmission
- drivetrain if relevant
- suspension
- suspension character
- running gear
- ban and wheel size
- fuel economy
- hill-climb ability
- NVH (noise, vibration, harshness)
- PEB / power back door / power tailgate (Ya/Tidak)
- spion retract (Ya/Tidak)
- kamera 360 (Ya/Tidak)
- adaptive cruise control or similar (Ya/Tidak)
- lane following assist / lane centering / similar (Ya/Tidak)
- collision-avoidance assist / autonomous emergency braking / similar (Ya/Tidak)
- full ADAS list
- annual tax
- after-sales support
- spare parts availability

When useful, also capture related information such as:

- seating capacity
- wheelbase
- brake type
- steering feel
- infotainment and connectivity
- airbag count
- hill-start assist / vehicle stability control
- service interval
- warranty
- resale value
- waiting period or network coverage

## Strengths and Weaknesses

List:

- strengths
- weaknesses

Tie each point to a specific variant when the point is not true for the whole model range.

## Driving and Comfort Assessment

Include evidence-based ratings with short justification. Use a consistent numeric scale such as 1-10.

Cover:

- comfort rating
- engine performance rating
- hill-climb ability
- NVH
- suspension behavior on city roads, broken roads, toll roads, and speed bumps when evidence exists

## Reviewer Insights

Summarize professional reviewer opinions. Separate facts from subjective impressions.

Highlight disagreement when reviewers do not fully agree.

## Owner Feedback

Include at least 10 user or owner reviews when available, each with a source.

Prefer real owners, long-term users, forum posts, social posts, marketplace reviews, or community discussions over generic comments.

For each review, capture variant if known and focus on useful signals such as:

- fuel consumption reality
- comfort in long trips
- reliability
- maintenance issues
- spare-part availability
- service experience
- climbing performance
- noise and vibration

## Market Position

Compare the car with direct competitors, including which variants offer stronger value, better comfort, more complete safety, or lower ownership cost.

## Final Insight

Provide a balanced conclusion with clear buyer-fit guidance.

Never exaggerate strengths or weaknesses. If an additional fact is important or related to the review, extract it even if it is not explicitly listed above.
